config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can be used to excuse injustice and abuse. In my view, a strong society is built by protecting basic individual rights first, then balancing them with the common good when there is a real and clear public need. From my perspective, many governments already lean too easily toward control, so I am cautious about giving society priority by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami penuh dengan contoh bagaimana kekuasaan yang dibenarkan atas nama “kebaikan bersama” bisa berubah menjadi pelanggaran hak sipil, jadi saya sangat curiga pada klaim yang terlalu umum seperti itu. Hak individu memang tidak absolut dalam semua situasi, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi sebelum negara atau mayoritas boleh membatasinya. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan dasar sambil menangani kebutuhan publik secara terbatas dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because putting society first by default easily becomes an excuse for the state or the majority to trample people who are weaker or unpopular. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used too casually to justify overreach, corruption, or silencing criticism, so I am very wary of that logic. The common good matters, but it should limit individual rights only in narrow, clearly necessary, and proportional cases, not as a general principle. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals quickly; it is one that protects basic rights while solving real public needs responsibly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan hanya karena mayoritas menginginkannya. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, pembungkaman, atau diskriminasi terhadap kelompok yang tidak populer. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar sempit, perlu, dan proporsional, bukan dijadikan aturan default. Masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kebebasan dasar, lalu baru menyeimbangkannya dengan kebutuhan bersama ketika ada alasan yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual by default, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From an Indonesian perspective, I am especially suspicious of “public interest” language, since it can be used to silence criticism, protect the powerful, or pressure ordinary people who have little influence. The common good matters, but it should justify limits on rights only when the need is clear, narrow, and proportional. If a society is healthy, it protects basic rights first and proves its case before asking individuals to sacrifice them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan korban default demi kepentingan masyarakat. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi cek kosong atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, diskriminasi, atau pembungkaman kelompok yang tidak populer. Kepentingan publik memang bisa membenarkan pembatasan tertentu, tetapi hanya jika benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan proporsional, bukan karena masyarakat merasa lebih nyaman mengorbankan orang per orang. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat menuntut pengorbanan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin dalam melindungi kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
